name: Node.js
description:
Install Node.js and npm in the development container.

Two methods are supported, selected by the 'method' option:

'nvm' (default) — installs the Node Version Manager (nvm) to 'nvm_dir' and uses it to install the requested Node.js version. Supports Linux (glibc and musl/Alpine), macOS, and any POSIX platform. On Alpine Linux, Node.js is compiled from source automatically when the '-s' flag is used (build dependencies are installed from the apk manifest). Node.js binaries are symlinked to /usr/local/bin (when running as root and symlink=true) for universal PATH availability.

'binary' — downloads the official prebuilt Node.js tarball from nodejs.org/dist and extracts it to 'prefix'. Fast and dependency-free. Supports Linux glibc (x64, arm64, armv7l) and macOS (x64, arm64); NOT compatible with Alpine Linux (musl). Integrity is verified against SHASUMS256.txt via SHA-256.

Version selection supports nvm-style aliases ('lts/*', '--lts', major number) for both methods, and exact semver strings. The default 'lts/*' installs the latest Active LTS release.

method.description:
Installation method.
'nvm' (default) — install via nvm (Node Version Manager). Installs nvm itself to 'nvm_dir', then uses it to install the requested Node.js version. Supports all platforms including Alpine (via source compilation with 'nvm install -s'). The nvm shell function is available after sourcing '$nvm_dir/nvm.sh'; node/npm/npx binaries are symlinked to /usr/local/bin.
'binary' — download and extract the official prebuilt Node.js tarball from nodejs.org/dist to 'prefix'. No nvm overhead. Does NOT support Alpine Linux (glibc-only binaries). Exits with a clear error on Alpine; use method=nvm instead.

version.description:
Version of Node.js to install.
'lts/*' (default) — latest Active LTS release. With method=nvm, the shell form 'lts/*' is passed to nvm install (always quoted internally to prevent glob expansion). With method=binary, the latest LTS entry is resolved from nodejs.org/dist/index.json.
'lts' — alias for 'lts/*'; normalized internally.
'latest' / 'node' — very latest release (may be a non-LTS Current release).
A major number (e.g. '22') — latest stable release in that major line.
An exact semver string (e.g. '24.11.1' or 'v24.11.1') — installs that specific version.
'none' — install nvm itself but do not install any Node.js version (method=nvm only). Useful when a .nvmrc or lifecycle script manages node version selection.

additional_versions.description:
Comma-separated list of additional Node.js versions to install via nvm alongside the primary 'version', without setting them as the nvm default.
Only applies when method=nvm. Uses the same version syntax as 'version' (major number, exact semver, or LTS alias).
Useful for testing across multiple Node.js versions in a single container or satisfying .nvmrc lookups for sub-projects.

nvm_version.description:
Version of nvm to install. Only used when method=nvm.
'latest' (default) — resolves the newest nvm release tag via the GitHub Releases API at install time.
An explicit tag (e.g. '0.40.4' or 'v0.40.4') — pins nvm to that exact release.
The installer downloads the nvm install.sh from the tagged commit URL; 'latest' uses the same mechanism after resolving the tag.

nvm_dir.description:
Directory where nvm is installed ($NVM_DIR). Only used when method=nvm.
Default: '/usr/local/share/nvm' — system-wide location suitable for root installs in containers.
May be set to any writable absolute path, e.g. '/opt/nvm' or a user-specific path like '/home/user/.nvm'.
When this differs from '/usr/local/share/nvm' (the containerEnv NVM_DIR value) and symlink=true, a symlink is created from '/usr/local/share/nvm' to the actual nvm_dir, keeping the containerEnv value valid without any extra configuration.

prefix.description:
Installation prefix for method=binary (Node.js binaries land in $prefix/bin). Only used when method=binary.
'auto' (default) — resolves to /usr/local when running as root, or $HOME/.local when running as non-root.
An explicit absolute path (e.g. '/opt/node') — used as-is; the script exits with an error if the path is not writable and cannot be created.

arch.description:
Override the CPU architecture used when selecting a prebuilt binary (method=binary only).
Accepted values: 'x86_64', 'aarch64' (mapped to linux-x64 / linux-arm64 for Linux; darwin-arm64 for macOS).
When empty (default), the architecture is detected automatically from uname -m.
Useful for cross-arch scenarios or forced emulation. Ignored when method=nvm.

installer_dir.description:
Temporary directory for downloading the Node.js tarball and checksum sidecar (method=binary) or the nvm install script (method=nvm).
All downloaded files are removed after a successful install.

if_exists.description:
What to do when a 'node' binary is already found in PATH before installation begins.
If the installed version already matches the resolved target version, installation is always skipped silently regardless of this setting.
'skip' (default) — log a notice and exit successfully without making any changes (idempotent).
'fail' — print an error and exit non-zero.
'reinstall' — remove the existing Node.js installation and install fresh using the selected method. With method=nvm, runs 'nvm uninstall <version>' then reinstalls. With method=binary, overwrites the extracted files.

export_path.description:
Controls which shell startup files receive PATH export blocks for the Node.js binary directory.
'auto' (default) — as root, writes to all system-wide shell startup files: /etc/environment (BASH_ENV and PATH), /etc/profile.d/install-node.sh (login shells), the system-wide bashrc (non-login interactive bash), and the zsh env file (zshenv). Covers login, interactive, non-interactive, Docker RUN, SSH exec, and PAM invocation scenarios.
'' (empty string) — skips all PATH writes (e.g. when prefix=/usr/local which is already on PATH, or when using symlinks only).
A newline-separated list of absolute file paths — writes only to those specific files.

users.description:
Comma-separated list of users. Serves two purposes:

1. Group membership (method=nvm, set_permissions=true): all listed users are added to the 'group' group (default: 'nvm'), giving them group-write access to 'nvm_dir' and the ability to run 'nvm install'/'nvm use' without sudo. The nvm installer is run as the first resolved user. When empty, defaults to the current running user (id -nu).

2. Per-user shell RC PATH exports: listed users also receive a PATH export block appended to their personal shell RC files (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.), in addition to any system-wide files written by 'export_path'.

Special tokens: '_REMOTE_USER' (devcontainer remoteUser, from $_REMOTE_USER), '_CONTAINER_USER' (devcontainer containerUser, from $_CONTAINER_USER), 'all' (current user + _REMOTE_USER + _CONTAINER_USER, deduplicated). Tokens and names may be mixed, e.g. '_REMOTE_USER,alice'.
As non-root, restricted to the current user only; other names are ignored with a warning.
Set to '' (default) to skip per-user RC writes and use the running user for group membership.

set_permissions.description:
Create a dedicated group (name set by 'group', default 'nvm'), set group-write and setgid bits on 'nvm_dir', add all users listed in 'users' to that group, and run the nvm installer as the first resolved user rather than root.
This allows non-root container users to run 'nvm install', 'nvm use', and 'nvm ls' without sudo inside the running container.
Only applies when method=nvm and running as root. No-op when method=binary or running as non-root.

group.description:
Name of the group created to own 'nvm_dir' when set_permissions=true.
All users listed in 'users' are added to this group.
Only applies when method=nvm and set_permissions=true.

symlink.description:
Create symlinks that ensure the containerEnv-declared paths always resolve to the actual installation (root only).

For method=nvm:
  - The feature sets NVM_SYMLINK_CURRENT=true (both in containerEnv and during install), which causes nvm to maintain a '$NVM_DIR/current' symlink always pointing to the active Node.js version. The containerEnv PATH includes '$NVM_DIR/current/bin', so 'nvm use' version switching works automatically for all container processes.
  - When nvm_dir differs from '/usr/local/share/nvm' (the containerEnv NVM_DIR value), creates a directory symlink /usr/local/share/nvm -> nvm_dir so that both containerEnv.NVM_DIR and the PATH entry remain valid without extra user configuration.

For method=binary:
  - Symlinks node, npm, npx, and corepack from $prefix/bin/ into /usr/local/bin only when prefix resolves to something other than /usr/local (otherwise they are already in /usr/local/bin).

No-op when running as non-root (cannot write to /usr/local/share or /usr/local/bin).

node_gyp_deps.description:
Install OS build dependencies required to compile native Node.js modules via node-gyp: 'make', 'gcc'/'g++', and 'python3'.
On Alpine Linux with method=nvm, these tools are already provided by the nvm source-build toolchain (nvm.yaml manifest) and this option is a no-op.
Set to false to skip installation of build tools if you do not need to compile native modules and prefer a smaller image.

pnpm_version.description:
Version of pnpm to install globally via 'npm install -g pnpm@VERSION' after Node.js is installed.
'none' (default) — skip pnpm installation.
'latest' — install the latest pnpm release.
An explicit version string (e.g. '9.15.0') — install that exact pnpm release.

yarn_version.description:
Version of Yarn to install globally after Node.js is installed.
'none' (default) — skip Yarn installation.
'latest' — enable Yarn via corepack (ships with Node.js 16+) using 'corepack enable'; falls back to 'npm install -g yarn' if corepack is unavailable.
An explicit version string (e.g. '1.22.22') — install that exact Yarn release via 'npm install -g yarn@VERSION'.

logfile.description: Append install log to this file path.

github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.instr.text: This dev container includes 'node', 'npm', and optionally 'pnpm'/'yarn' pre-installed and available on the PATH for Node.js development.
